channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: Microsoft Defender Bug Triggers False Malware Alerts for DigiCert Certificates
item.description: <p>Microsoft fixed a Defender false positive that flagged legitimate DigiCert certificates as malware, disrupting Windows trust stores for some IT teams.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-microsoft-defender-digicert-certificates-false-positive/">Microsoft Defender Bug Triggers False Malware Alerts for DigiCert Certificates</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: RMM Tools Fuel Stealthy Phishing Campaign
item.description: Attackers are abusing two remote monitoring and management (RMM) tools to evade detection in a campaign that has impacted over 80 organizations so far.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Exploit Cyber-Frenzy Threatens Millions via Critical cPanel Vulnerability
item.description: Shortly after the authentication-bypass flaw was disclosed multiple proof-of-concept exploits appeared, and one researcher claims there's been zero-day activity for at least a month.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: Cisco Moves to Acquire Astrix Security to Tackle Non-Human Identity Risks
item.description: <p>The acquisition strengthens Cisco’s push into identity-centric security for AI and machine access.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/cisco-moves-to-acquire-astrix-security-to-tackle-non-human-identity-risks/">Cisco Moves to Acquire Astrix Security to Tackle Non-Human Identity Risks</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Mike Lennon

item.title: Phishing Campaign Hits 80+ Orgs Using SimpleHelp and ScreenConnect RMM Tools
item.description: An active phishing campaign has been observed targeting multiple vectors since at least April 2025, with legitimate Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) software as a way to establish persistent remote access to compromised hosts. The activity, codenamed VENOMOUS#HELPER, has impacted over 80 organizations, most of which are in the U.S., according to Securonix. It shares overlaps with clusters
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Indirect Prompt Injection Is Now a Real-World AI Security Threat
item.description: <p>AI agents are now being weaponized through prompt injection, exposing why model guardrails are not enough to protect enterprise data.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-ai-agents-prompt-injection-data-security/">Indirect Prompt Injection Is Now a Real-World AI Security Threat</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Tim Freestone

item.title: Trellix Source Code Repository Breached
item.description: <p>The cybersecurity firm’s investigation has not found any impact on its source code release or distribution process. </p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/trellix-source-code-repository-breached/">Trellix Source Code Repository Breached</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Progress Patches Critical MOVEit Automation Bug Enabling Authentication Bypass
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Silver Fox Springs Tax-Themed Attacks on Orgs in India, Russia
item.description: More than 1,600 socially engineered messages from the China-backed advanced persistent threat (APT) group target various sectors to deliver the previously undocumented ABCDoor backdoor, ValleyRAT, and other malware.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: AI-Powered Phishing, Android Spying Tool, Linux Exploit, GitHub RCE & More
item.description: This week, the shadows moved faster than the patches. While most teams were still triaging last month’s alerts, attackers had already turned control panels into kill switches, kernels into open doors, and open-source pipelines into silent delivery systems. The game has shifted from breach to occupation. They’re living inside SaaS sessions, pushing code with trusted commits, and scaling
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Cybersecurity M&A Roundup: 33 Deals Announced in April 2026
item.description: <p>Significant cybersecurity M&#038;A deals announced by Airbus, Cyera, Fortra, Palo Alto Networks, Silverfort, and Socket.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/cybersecurity-ma-roundup-33-deals-announced-in-april-2026/">Cybersecurity M&#038;A Roundup: 33 Deals Announced in April 2026</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: DigiCert Revokes Certificates After Support Portal Hack
item.description: <p>Hackers delivered malware via a customer chat channel, infected an analyst’s system, and accessed the internal support portal.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/digicert-revokes-certificates-after-support-portal-hack/">DigiCert Revokes Certificates After Support Portal Hack</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Canvas Breach May Put 275M Users, 9,000 Schools at Risk
item.description: <p>Instructure confirms a Canvas breach involving user information and messages as hackers claim 275M users and nearly 9,000 schools were affected.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-canvas-instructure-breach-275m-users/">Canvas Breach May Put 275M Users, 9,000 Schools at Risk</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Liz Ticong

item.title: Small Defense Firms Lack Network Data to Stop Nation-State Hackers, Analyst Says
item.description: Team Cymru’s Stephen Campbell warned that small US defense contractors are not well prepared to face cyber intrusions through edge devices

item.title: Stronger Cybersecurity, Stronger Business: NIST Celebrates 2026 National Small Business Week
item.description: Happy National Small Business Week! For over 60 years, the U.S. Small Business Administration has led this initiative to acknowledge the critical contributions of America’s entrepreneurs and small business owners. Part of the U.S. Department of Commerce, NIST’s mission is to drive U.S. innovation and global competitiveness, and the small business community is central to this mission. In this year’s blog, we shine a spotlight on some new and upcoming NIST resources that are all focused on strengthening the cybersecurity and resilience of the nation’s small business community. Build Your Small
item.dc:creator: Daniel Eliot

item.title: 2026: The Year of AI-Assisted Attacks
item.description: On December 4, 2025, a 17-year-old was arrested in Osaka under Japan’s Unauthorized Access Prohibition Act. The young man had run malicious code to extract the personal data of over 7 million users of Kaikatsu Club, Japan's largest internet cafe chain. When asked, the young man shared his motivation for the hack: he wanted to buy Pokémon cards. In a sense, this is a fairly conventional story.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Silver Fox Deploys ABCDoor Malware via Tax-Themed Phishing in India and Russia
item.description: The China-based cybercrime group known as Silver Fox has been linked to a new campaign targeting organizations in Russia and India with a new malware called ABCDoor. The activity involved using phishing emails that mimic correspondence from the Income Tax Department of India in December 2025, followed by a similar campaign aimed at Russian entities. "Both waves followed a nearly identical
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: How Dark Reading Lifted Off the Launchpad in 2006
item.description: Twenty years ago, this media brand didn't have a print edition to attract eyeballs and sponsors. Top-notch content and editorial talent did the heavy lifting.
item.dc:creator: Terry Sweeney

item.title: Exploitation of ‘Copy Fail’ Linux Vulnerability Begins
item.description: <p>CISA has added the bug to its KEV list, and Microsoft has observed limited exploitation, mainly associated with PoC testing.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/exploitation-of-copy-fail-linux-vulnerability-begins/">Exploitation of &#8216;Copy Fail&#8217; Linux Vulnerability Begins</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Hacking Polymarket
item.description: <p>Polymarket is a platform where people can bet on real-world events, political and otherwise. Leaving the ethical considerations of this aside (for one, it facilitates <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assassination_market">assassination</a>), one of the issues with making this work is the verification of these real-world events. Polymarket gamblers have <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/mar/18/polymarket-gamblers-threaten-israeli-journalist-missile-strike-wager">threatened</a> a journalist because his story was being used to verify an event. And now, gamblers are taking <a href="https://www.engadget.com/big-tech/someone-allegedly-used-a-hairdryer-to-rig-polymarket-weather-bets-155312411.html">hair dryers</a> to weather sensors to rig weather bets.</p> <p>There&#8217;s also <a href="https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c20832yg5p2o">insider trading</a>: a <a href="https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cge0grppe3po">lot of it</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: OpenAI Rolls Out Advanced Security for ChatGPT Accounts
item.description: <p>Advanced Account Security provides stronger login methods, more secure account recovery, shorter sessions, and training exclusion.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/openai-rolls-out-advanced-security-for-chatgpt-accounts/">OpenAI Rolls Out Advanced Security for ChatGPT Accounts</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Critical cPanel Vulnerability Weaponized to Target Government and MSP Networks
item.description: A previously unknown threat actor has been observed targeting government and military entities in Southeast Asia, alongside a smaller cluster of managed service providers (MSPs) and hosting providers in the Philippines, Laos, Canada, South Africa, and the U.S., by exploiting the recently disclosed vulnerability in cPanel. The activity, detected by Ctrl-Alt-Intel on May 2, 2026, involves the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Over 40,000 Servers Compromised in Ongoing cPanel Exploitation
item.description: <p>The attacks likely target CVE-2026-41940, a recently patched zero-day leading to administrative access.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/over-40000-servers-compromised-in-ongoing-cpanel-exploitation/">Over 40,000 Servers Compromised in Ongoing cPanel Exploitation</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: OpenAI To Extend Cyber Program to Government Agencies
item.description: OpenAI announced its intention to expand the Trusted Access for Cyber program for cyber defenders at the federal, state and local government levels

item.title: Edtech Firm Instructure Discloses Data Breach Amid Hacker Leak Threats
item.description: <p>Hackers disrupted services and stole names, email addresses, student ID numbers, and user messages.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/edtech-firm-instructure-discloses-data-breach/">Edtech Firm Instructure Discloses Data Breach Amid Hacker Leak Threats</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Global Crackdown Arrests 276, Shuts 9 Crypto Scam Centers, Seizes $701M
item.description: A coordinated international operation involving U.S. and Chinese authorities has arrested at least 276 suspects and shut down nine scam centers used for cryptocurrency investment fraud schemes targeting Americans, resulting in millions of dollars in losses. The crackdown was led by the Dubai Police, under the United Arab Emirates (UAE) Ministry of Interior, in partnership with the U.S. Federal
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: US Military Reaches Deals With 7 Tech Companies to Use Their AI on Classified Systems
item.description: <p>Google, Microsoft, Amazon Web Services, Nvidia, OpenAI, Reflection and SpaceX will provide resources to help augment warfighter decision-making in complex operational environments,” the Defense Department said.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/us-military-reaches-deals-with-7-tech-companies-to-use-their-ai-on-classified-systems/">US Military Reaches Deals With 7 Tech Companies to Use Their AI on Classified Systems</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Associated Press

item.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited Linux Root Access Bug CVE-2026-31431 to KEV
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Friday added a recently disclosed security flaw impacting various Linux distributions to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-31431 (CVSS score: 7.8), is a case of local privilege escalation (LPE) flaw that could allow an
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New Bluekit Phishing Kit Features AI Assistant
item.description: <p>Still under development, Bluekit provides users with automated domain registration and an AI Assistant.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/new-bluekit-phishing-kit-features-ai-assistant/">New Bluekit Phishing Kit Features AI Assistant</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Trellix Confirms Source Code Breach With Unauthorized Repository Access
item.description: Cybersecurity company Trellix has announced that it suffered a breach that enabled unauthorized access to a "portion" of its source code. It said it "recently identified" the compromise of its source code repository and that it began working with "leading forensic experts" to resolve the matter immediately. It also said it has notified law enforcement of the matter. Trellix did not disclose the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 76% of All Crypto Stolen in 2026 Is Now in North Korea
item.description: North Korean threat actors are pulling off historic cryptocurrency heists on a yearly, sometimes weekly basis now. AI might be helping them.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: 30,000 Facebook Accounts Hacked via Google AppSheet Phishing Campaign
item.description: A newly discovered Vietnamese-linked operation has been observed using a Google AppSheet as a "phishing relay" to distribute phishing emails with an aim to compromise Facebook accounts. The activity has been codenamed AccountDumpling by Guardio, with the scheme selling the stolen accounts back through an illicit storefront run by the threat actors. In all, roughly 30,000 Facebook accounts are
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: OpenAI Introduces Password-Free Login for Millions of ChatGPT Users
item.description: <p>OpenAI’s Advanced Account Security lets ChatGPT and Codex users replace passwords with passkeys or security keys, but recovery is limited.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-openai-chatgpt-advanced-account-security-passkeys/">OpenAI Introduces Password-Free Login for Millions of ChatGPT Users</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Microsoft Flagged 8.3B Phishing Emails in Q1 as QR Codes, CAPTCHAs Rise
item.description: <p>Microsoft flagged 8.3 billion phishing emails as attackers turned to QR codes, fake CAPTCHAs, PhaaS kits, and file-based payloads.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-microsoft-phishing-emails-qr-codes-captcha-phaas/">Microsoft Flagged 8.3B Phishing Emails in Q1 as QR Codes, CAPTCHAs Rise</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: If AI's So Smart, Why Does It Keep Deleting Production Databases?
item.description: The issue isn't artificial intelligence, but rather an industry adding AI agent integrations into production environments before proper security testing.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Cybercrime Groups Using Vishing and SSO Abuse in Rapid SaaS Extortion Attacks
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are warning of two cybercrime groups that are carrying out "rapid, high-impact attacks" operating almost within the confines of SaaS environments, while leaving minimal traces of their actions. The clusters, Cordial Spider (aka BlackFile, CL-CRI-1116, O-UNC-045, and UNC6671) and Snarky Spider (aka O-UNC-025 and UNC6661), have been attributed to high-speed data theft and
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: China-Linked Hackers Target Asian Governments, NATO State, Journalists, and Activists
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new China-aligned espionage campaign targeting government and defense sectors across South, East, and Southeast Asia, along with one European government belonging to NATO. Trend Micro has attributed the activity to a threat activity cluster it tracks under the temporary designation SHADOW-EARTH-053. The adversarial collective is assessed to
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: AI Power Plays, Security Breaches, and Industry Shifts Define the Week in Tech
item.description: <p>See what you missed in Daily Tech Insider from April 27–May 1.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/ai-power-plays-security-breaches-and-industry-shifts-define-the-week-in-tech/">AI Power Plays, Security Breaches, and Industry Shifts Define the Week in Tech</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Name That Toon: Mark of (Security) Progress
item.description: Feeling creative? Have something to say about the last 20 years of cybersecurity? Our editors will award the best cybersecurity-related caption with a $20 gift card.
item.dc:creator: John Klossner

item.title: 20 Years in Cyber: Dark Reading Marks Milestone With Month of Special Coverage
item.description: On this day in 2006, Dark Reading went live. We have a celebration planned that spans our two decades of covering the industry, and you, dear readers, are invited.
item.dc:creator: Kelly Jackson Higgins

item.title: Anthropic Rolls Out Claude Security for AI Vulnerability Scanning
item.description: Claude Security enters public beta, giving enterprises AI driven code scanning with no API integration or custom agents required

item.title: Two American Cybersecurity Workers Jailed for BlackCat Ransomware Attacks
item.description: The cybersecurity workers used their knowledge and skills to conduct ransomware attacks for notorious gang, rather than protect victims against them

item.title: A Ransomware Negotiator Was Working for a Ransomware Gang
item.description: <p>Someone <a href="https://gizmodo.com/a-ransomware-negotiator-pleads-guilty-to-being-a-double-agent-2000749234">pleaded guilty</a> to secretly working for a ransomware gang as he negotiated ransomware payments for clients.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Nine-Year-Old Zero-Day Flaw in Linux Kernel Discovered by AI-Equipped Security Researcher
item.description: A researcher from offensive security firm Theori has found a nine-year-old flaw in the Linux kernel with the help of AI

item.title: Top Five Sales Challenges Costing MSPs Cybersecurity Revenue
item.description: The managed security services market is projected to grow from $38.31 billion in 2025 to $69.16 billion by 2030[1], with cybersecurity being the fastest-growing sector[2]. Despite this opportunity, many MSPs leave revenue on the table because their go-to-market strategy fails to connect technical expertise with business needs. This execution gap is where most deals stall. MSPs often focus on
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Two Cybersecurity Professionals Get 4-Year Sentences in BlackCat Ransomware Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Thursday announced the sentencing of two cybersecurity professionals to four years each in prison for their role in facilitating BlackCat ransomware attacks in 2023. Ryan Goldberg, 40, of Georgia, and Kevin Martin, 36, of Texas, were accused of deploying the ransomware against multiple victims located throughout the U.S. between April and December 2023.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Poisoned Ruby Gems and Go Modules Exploit CI Pipelines for Credential Theft
item.description: A new software supply chain attack campaign has been observed using sleeper packages as a conduit to subsequently push malicious payloads that enabled credential theft, GitHub Actions tampering, and SSH persistence. The activity has been attributed to the GitHub account "BufferZoneCorp," which has published a set of repositories that are associated with malicious Ruby gems and Go modules. As of
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Billions of Chrome Users Urged to Update After Google Patches 30 Security Flaws
item.description: <p>Google patched 30 Chrome vulnerabilities, including four Critical flaws. Here’s what users should know and how to update Chrome and Firefox.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-google-chrome-security-update-30-vulnerabilities-april-2026/">Billions of Chrome Users Urged to Update After Google Patches 30 Security Flaws</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: TeamPCP Hits SAP Packages With 'Mini Shai-Hulud' Attack
item.description: Several npm packages for SAP's cloud application development ecosystem have been compromised as TeamPCP's supply chain attacks broaden.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: Samsung’s Free Android Upgrade Brings Better Security to Galaxy Phones
item.description: <p>Samsung’s One UI 8.5 update may bring stronger Galaxy security controls as users report battery drain and overheating after recent patches.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-samsung-one-ui-8-5-galaxy-security-battery-drain/">Samsung’s Free Android Upgrade Brings Better Security to Galaxy Phones</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Esther Shein

item.title: Another AI-Assisted Software Scan Yields 9-Year-Old Linux Bug
item.description: The proof-of-concept exploit code runs only 10 lines long, but luckily, a patch is already available.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Cisco Introduces Model Provenance Kit to Strengthen AI Supply Chain Security
item.description: <p>Cisco’s open-source Model Provenance Kit helps organizations verify AI model origins, trace lineage, and reduce AI supply chain security risks.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-cisco-model-provenance-kit-ai-supply-chain-security/">Cisco Introduces Model Provenance Kit to Strengthen AI Supply Chain Security</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Anthropic's Mythos Has Landed: Here's What Comes Next for Cyber
item.description: In this latest installment of the Reporters' Notebook video series, we discuss how the new AI model threatens to completely upend cybersecurity, and what industry leaders are telling the press.
item.dc:creator: Becky Bracken, Kristina Beek

item.title: PyTorch Lightning and Intercom-client Hit in Supply Chain Attacks to Steal Credentials
item.description: In yet another software supply chain attack, threat actors have managed to compromise the popular Python package Lightning to push two malicious versions to conduct credential theft. According to Aikido Security, OX Security, Socket, and StepSecurity, the two malicious versions are versions 2.6.2 and 2.6.3, both of which were published on April 30, 2026. The campaign is assessed to be an
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: MCP: The AI Protocol Quietly Expanding Your Attack Surface | UpGuard
item.description: As AI tools adopt MCP, security teams face a new shadow IT crisis. Learn what MCP is, why these servers carry risk, and how to begin gaining visibility.

item.title: Three Arrested for Hacking Over 610,000 Roblox Accounts
item.description: Suspects accused of distributing malware and selling access to stolen Roblox accounts on Russian marketplaces

item.title: Deep#Door Python Backdoor Evades Detection On Windows
item.description: Deep#Door Python RAT uses tunneling and obfuscation to evade detection and steal credentials

item.title: Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
item.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: CISA and Partners Publish Zero Trust Guidance For OT Security
item.description: A new CISA‑led guide explains how zero‑trust security can be applied to operational technology, balancing cyber defence with safety and system availability

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: SMS Blaster Busts, OpenEMR Flaws, 600K Roblox Hacks and 25 More Stories
item.description: The internet is noisy this week. We are seeing some wild new tactics, like people using fake cell towers to send scam texts, while some developers are accidentally downloading tools that peek into their private files during a simple install. It is definitely a busy time to be online. Security is always a moving target. Millions of servers are currently sitting online without any passwords, and
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Oracle Red Bull Racing Team Revs Up Automation to Boost Security
item.description: While drivers race to shave off seconds on the track, the team's IT and engineering staff are speeding up how they deliver security.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: UK: Education Sector Faces Surge in Cyber Breaches Despite Stable National Threat Levels
item.description: The British public education sector has faced the nation’s most dramatic increase in cyber breach prevalence over the past year

item.title: New Python Backdoor Uses Tunneling Service to Steal Browser and Cloud Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a stealthy Python-based backdoor framework called DEEP#DOOR that comes with capabilities to establish persistent access and harvest a wide range of sensitive information from compromised hosts. "The intrusion chain begins with execution of a batch script ('install_obf.bat') that disables Windows security controls, dynamically extracts an
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: EtherRAT Distribution Spoofing Administrative Tools via GitHub Facades
item.description: Intro A sophisticated, high-resilience malicious campaign was identified by Atos Threat Research Center (TRC) in March 2026. This operation specifically targets the high-privilege professional accounts of enterprise administrators, DevOps engineers, and security analysts by impersonating administrative utilities they rely on for daily operations. By integrating&nbsp;Search Engine Order (SEO)
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Fast16 Malware
item.description: <p>Researchers have reverse-engineered a piece of malware named Fast16. It&#8217;s almost certainly state-sponsored, probably US in origin, and was <a href="https://www.wired.com/story/fast16-malware-stuxnet-precursor-iran-nuclear-attack/?_sp=72d58355-e351-43ad-ba73-bc2b546a30a0.1777128353268">deployed</a> against Iran years before Stuxnet:</p> <blockquote><p>&#8220;&#8230;the Fast16 malware was designed to carry out the most subtle form of sabotage ever seen in an in-the-wild malware tool: By automatically spreading across networks and then silently manipulating computation processes in certain software applications that perform high-precision mathematical calculations and simulate physical phenomena, Fast16 can alter the results of those programs to cause failures that range from faulty research results to catastrophic damage to real-world equipment.&#8221;...</p></blockquote>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Europol Busts Albanian Scam Call Centers in Major Online Fraud Case
item.description: European police arrested 10 suspects after dismantling Albanian scam call centers linked to a €50m ($58m) online investment fraud operation

item.title: New Linux 'Copy Fail' Vulnerability Enables Root Access on Major Distributions
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a Linux local privilege escalation (LPE) flaw that could allow an unprivileged local user to obtain root. The high-severity vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-31431 (CVSS score: 7.8) has been codenamed Copy Fail by Xint.io and Theori. "An unprivileged local user can write four controlled bytes into the page cache of any readable file on a Linux
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Cyber is the Number One Global “People Risk,” Says Marsh
item.description: Marsh’s 2026 People Risks survey finds cyber‑related challenges dominate, as cyber‑threat literacy tops risks and cyber and AI skills shortages rise

item.title: This month in security with Tony Anscombe – April 2026 edition
item.description: Warnings about helpdesk impersonation scams and Iran-linked hackers targeting critical sectors in the US, plus the most damaging scams of 2025 - here's some of what made the headlines this month

item.title: Google Fixes CVSS 10 Gemini CLI CI RCE and Cursor Flaws Enable Code Execution
item.description: Google has addressed a maximum severity security flaw in Gemini CLI -- the "@google/gemini-cli" npm package and the "google-github-actions/run-gemini-cli" GitHub Actions workflow -- that could have allowed attackers to execute arbitrary commands on host systems. "The vulnerability allowed an unprivileged external attacker to force their own malicious content to load as Gemini configuration,"
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Microsoft Confirms Windows Flaw Is Being Exploited After Incomplete Patch
item.description: <p>Microsoft confirmed a Windows zero-click flaw tied to an incomplete patch is being exploited, putting credentials at risk for unpatched users.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-microsoft-windows-zero-click-flaw-incomplete-patch/">Microsoft Confirms Windows Flaw Is Being Exploited After Incomplete Patch</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Claude Mythos Fears Startle Japan's Financial Services Sector
item.description: Global financial institutions are panicked over Anthropic's new superhacker AI model. Cyber experts aren't quite as worried.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Reverse Engineering With AI Unearths High-Severity GitHub Bug
item.description: Wiz used an AI reverse-engineering tool to pinpoint a vulnerability that previously would have been too costly and time-consuming to undertake.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Hackers Abuse Robinhood Signup Process to Deliver Phishing Emails
item.description: <p>Robinhood fixed an account-creation flaw that hackers abused to send convincing phishing emails from its own system to some users over the weekend.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-robinhood-phishing-emails-official-address/">Hackers Abuse Robinhood Signup Process to Deliver Phishing Emails</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: AI Finds 38 Security Flaws in Electronic Health Record Platform
item.description: Flaws in OpenEMR's platform — used by more than 100,000 healthcare providers — enabled database compromise, remote code execution, and data theft.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: SAS Launches AI Governance Tools to Tame Agentic AI in the Enterprise
item.description: <p>SAS expands Viya with governed AI agents, copilots, and new governance tools aimed at helping enterprises manage shadow AI and build trust in automation.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-sas-agentic-ai-governance-tools/">SAS Launches AI Governance Tools to Tame Agentic AI in the Enterprise</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Drew Robb

item.title: The Shadow Supply Chain: A Pivot To Usage-Based Discovery | UpGuard
item.description: Pivot from "purchase-based" to usage-based discovery. See how browser telemetry and identity logs work together to secure your true vendor footprint.

item.title: 1 in 15 MCP Servers are Lookalikes: Is Your Org at Risk? | UpGuard
item.description: For every official MCP server, up to 15 lookalikes exist. Learn to identify these registry-layer threats and discover methods to protect your organization.

item.title: The Context Gap: How Nearly Half of Your Time is Lost to Investigation | UpGuard
item.description: Security teams waste 43% of their response time on manual context gathering, making high-context intelligence a necessity in modern cybersecurity.

item.title: SAP-Related npm Packages Compromised in Credential-Stealing Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are sounding the alarm about a new supply chain attack campaign targeting SAP-related npm Packages with credential-stealing malware. According to reports from Aikido Security, Onapsis, OX Security,&nbsp;SafeDep, Socket, StepSecurity, and Google-owned Wiz, the campaign –&nbsp;calling itself the Mini&nbsp;Shai-Hulud – has affected the following packages associated with
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The Shadow Supply Chain: The SSO & AI Visibility Gap | UpGuard
item.description: Is your SSO enough? 31.4% of vendor interactions bypass identity logs. Discover how Shadow AI and direct logins redefine vendor visibility.

item.title: Vect 2.0 Ransomware Acts as Wiper, Thanks to Design Error
item.description: The emerging ransomware has been deployed against victims of the TeamPCP supply chain attacks, but organizations should think twice before paying for a decryptor.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Cursor Extension Flaw Exposes Developer API Keys
item.description: Cursor flaw lets extensions steal API keys and session tokens without user interaction, according to researchers at LayerX

item.title: New Wave of DPRK Attacks Uses AI-Inserted npm Malware, Fake Firms, and RATs
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered malicious code in an npm package after a malicious package as a dependency to the project by Anthropic's Claude Opus large language model (LLM). The package in question is "@validate-sdk/v2," which is listed on npm as a utility software development kit (SDK) for hashing, validation, encoding/decoding, and secure random generation. However, its real
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Malicious npm Dependency Linked to AI Assisted Commit Targets Crypto Wallets
item.description: Researchers uncover a malicious npm dependency linked to an AI‑assisted code commit that steals sensitive data and exposes crypto wallets

item.title: What Is Dark Web Monitoring? | UpGuard
item.description: Understand what dark web monitoring is, and why it’s essential for third-party risk management. Learn how UpGuard helps detect exposed data in real-time.

item.title: Lotus Wiper Attack Targets Venezuelan Energy Firms, Utilities
item.description: An analysis of the destructive malware reveals sophisticated living-off-the-land (LotL) techniques and detailed strategies for the widespread deletion of data.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Researchers Track 2.9 Billion Compromised Credentials
item.description: KELA claims infostealers remained the primary access vector for attacks in 2025

item.title: Webinar: How to Automate Exposure Validation to Match the Speed of AI Attacks
item.description: In February 2026, researchers uncovered a shift that completely changed the game: threat actors are now using custom AI setups to automate attacks directly into the kill chain. We aren't just talking about AI writing better phishing emails anymore. We’re talking about autonomous agents mapping Active Directory and seizing Domain Admin credentials in minutes. The problem? Most defensive workflows
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Claude Mythos Fears Startle Japan's Financial Services Sector
What to Look for in an Exposure Management Platform (And What Most of Them Get Wrong)
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Critical Flaw Turns Vect Ransomware into Data Destroying Wiper
item.description: The Vect 2.0 ransomware wipes large files instead of merely encrypting them, making recovery impossible – even for the attackers

item.title: Claude Mythos Has Found 271 Zero-Days in Firefox
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: A Quarter of Healthcare Organizations Report Medical Device Cyber-Attacks
item.description: RunSafe report reveals most attacks on medical devices disrupt patient care

item.title: Critical cPanel Authentication Vulnerability Identified — Update Your Server Immediately
item.description: cPanel has released security updates to address a security issue impacting various authentication paths that could allow an attacker to obtain access to the control panel software. The problem affects all currently supported versions of cPanel and WebHost Manager (WHM), according to an alert published by WebPros on Tuesday. It does not have an official identifier. The issue has been addressed in
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited ConnectWise and Windows Flaws to KEV
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added two security flaws impacting ConnectWise ScreenConnect and Microsoft Windows to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation. The vulnerabilities are listed below -
CVE-2024-1708 (CVSS score: 8.4) - A path traversal vulnerability in&nbsp; ConnectWise ScreenConnect
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: LiteLLM CVE-2026-42208 SQL Injection Exploited within 36 Hours of Disclosure
item.description: In yet another instance of threat actors quickly jumping on the exploitation bandwagon, a newly disclosed critical security flaw in BerriAI's LiteLLM Python package has come under active exploitation in the wild within 36 hours of the bug becoming public knowledge. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-42208 (CVSS score: 9.3), is an SQL injection that could be exploited to modify the underlying
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: BlueNoroff Uses Fake Zoom Calls to Turn Victims Into Attack Lures
item.description: The North Korean group is using stolen victim videos, AI-generated avatars, and fake Zoom calls to scale malware attacks against cryptocurrency executives.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: ClickUp Data Leak Exposes Enterprise Emails for Over a Year
item.description: <p>A hardcoded ClickUp API key exposed hundreds of corporate and government emails for over a year, raising new SaaS security concerns.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-clickup-api-key-email-exposure/">ClickUp Data Leak Exposes Enterprise Emails for Over a Year</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: NSA Chief During Snowden Affair Shares Regrets, Reflections 13 Years Later
item.description: Chris Inglis was the head civilian in charge at the NSA when the Snowden leaks exploded. He gets candid about mistakes the organization made, and what CISOs need to know about spotting potential threats, media disclosures, and &quot;enculturation.&quot;
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading Editorial Team
